reasoning: The informal statement asserts that every uncountable subset E ⊆ ℝ has a condensation point, where "E condenses at p" means every neighborhood of p meets E in an uncountable set. The Lean signature takes `hE : ¬ Set.Countable E` (i.e., E is uncountable — uncountability in classical math standardly means "not countable") and concludes `∃ p : ℝ, ∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)`, which directly expresses "there exists p ∈ ℝ such that every neighborhood U of p satisfies U ∩ E is uncountable." The use of the neighborhood filter `𝓝 p` with the quantifier `∀ U ∈ 𝓝 p` is the standard Mathlib encoding of "for every neighborhood U of p," and `Set.Countable` matches the standard countability notion. The hypothesis and conclusion types and quantifier structure match the informal statement exactly.
